shared_properties: The overlay uses project colors only and stays compatible with full-screen UI compositing in Bevy.
Authoring assumes a 1920x1080 orthographic target with centered gameplay framing.
The preview exists to clarify puzzle logic and therefore must preserve readability of existing HUD and world silhouettes.
differences: Unlike persistent HUD panels, this asset is an optional full-screen help layer tied to one explicit input.
Only the active frame carries amber beam guides; the hidden frame is truly empty to avoid false positives.
The overlay highlights predicted paths and impact zones rather than live damage or state numbers.

reference_images.label: gdd_markdown
reference_images.purpose: Grounds the shadow survival loop, mirror and prism puzzle rules, scale targets, HUD placements, and Bevy runtime constraints for Umbra Scale.

reference_images.label: moodboard
reference_images.purpose: Grounds the twilight art deco tower mood, silhouette contrast, gold trims, and hard-edge lighting style.

bevy_constraints.asset_loading: Load light_path_preview_overlay.png during Loading and expose frame 0 as hidden plus frame 1 as the active overlay used when Tab toggles path preview on.
runtime_notes: Render the overlay in UI space above the world and below modal pause or results panels.
Keep the active frame semi-transparent so beam guides stay visible without erasing the player, scale, or HUD.
Bind visibility to the Tab toggle only; no gameplay system should silently force the preview on.

context.usage: Covers the full screen with a 50% amber preview of light trajectories and beam-impact regions when the player requests path help.

layout_notes: Frame 0 is fully transparent and frame 1 reserves 50% opacity beam channels with no opaque fill blocks.
Leave the outer 48 px edges lighter than the center so the overlay does not compete with HUD anchors.
Beam guide strokes should remain narrower than 48 px to match the GDD hazard beam width.

acceptance_criteria: Atlas canvas is exactly 3840x1080 px for two 1920x1080 frames with no frame gutter.
The active frame averages approximately 50% opacity and never exceeds 80% opacity in any non-beam region.
HUD text and the Umbra silhouette remain readable when the active frame is composited over representative gameplay mockups.
